stallName: Siam Road Charcoal Char Koay Teow
description: Legendary uncle cooking charcoal char koay teow by the roadside. Expect long queues for his wok hei-infused masterpiece.
One of the most popular Nasi Kandar spots in Penang. Famous for their fried chicken and rich, mixed curries (kuah campur).
Refreshing, icy chendul packed with green jelly noodles, red beans, fresh coconut milk, and rich gula melaka.
The oldest Nasi Kandar restaurant in Penang (since 1907). World-famous for its perfectly spiced curries and murtabak.
A heritage stall run by two elderly sisters. Offers authentic Penang curry mee with cuttlefish and fragrant sambal.
Fiery, spicy, and satisfying nasi lemak wrapped in banana leaves. Located at Sri Weld Food Court.
Operating in an unassuming alley 24/7, serving heavily spiced curry, giant squids, and massive fish heads.
Iconic breakfast spot serving hot, crispy roti canai flooded with thick beef or chicken curry and a side of half-boiled eggs.
Hearty, deeply flavored Indian soups including ox-tail and mutton soup, served with crusty Bengali bread.
Night-time hawker stall famous for springy egg noodles tossed in dark soy sauce, topped with char siew and wontons.
Located on Presgrave Street, they serve rich and sweet prawn broth noodles packed with roasted pork and prawn slices.
Alleyway kopitiam famous for charcoal-toasted bread, half-boiled eggs, and thick robust Hainanese coffee.
Located right next to Kapitan Keling Mosque. Only opens at 10 PM and people line up early for the legendary curries.
Famed 24-hour joint specializing in sizzling Claypot Briyani and Tandoori chicken smothered in aromatic spices.
A hidden gem serving char koay teow with fresh duck egg and massive prawns. Packed with wok-hei.
Specializes in fried radish cake (Koay Kak) seasoned with soy sauce, preserved radish, and bean sprouts.
The ultimate Malaysian salad! Fresh seafood, fritters, and veggies drenched in a sweet and spicy nut sauce loaded with flavors.
Comforting flat rice noodles served in a clear, flavorful duck and pork broth topped with homemade eel fish balls.
Nostalgic coffee shop famous for its homemade durian ice cream and old-school Ais Kacang.
Traditional push-cart dim sum experience in the heart of George Town. Classic Siew Mai and Har Gao.
The famous stall operated by the lady in the red hat. Known for huge, juicy prawns and fiery noodles.
Generations-old Nyonya kuih factory turned cafe. Sells spectacular artisan traditional sweet and savory treats.
Famed sweet, airy, and soft Nyonya Apom completely cooked over charcoal stoves since the 1960s.
A massive, mouthwatering selection of spicy Minangkabau curries and side dishes located near Transfer Road.
Legendary Nasi Kandar establishment specifically revered for their rich, unadulterated Mutton Curry and Rose Chicken.
Nostalgic stall famous for hearty Bak Chang (meat dumplings) filled with pork, mushroom, salted egg, and chestnuts.
An elegant setting offering rare Jawi Peranakan dishes like Bamiyah (lamb stew) and Nasi Lemuni.
The iconic streetside Roti Bakar stacked and soaked heavily in beef curry or dhal, beloved by locals for hearty breakfasts.
Popular local breakfast spot in Lip Sin area known for comforting pork noodles and rich kopi-o.
Perfectly roasted and steamed chicken served atop fragrant oiled rice, a staple for quick hawker meals.
Decades-old kopitiam with rotating hawker stalls including famous fried oyster omelet and ice kacang.
Heritage Penang shop famous for butter cookies, tau sar pneah (green bean pastry), and mung bean cakes.
Old-school hawker center classic. Thick yellow noodles fried in rich dark soy with crispy pork lard and cockles.
Authentic Assam Laksa from the rustic Balik Pulau district. Sour, spicy, and aromatic fish-based soup.
Traditional Malay herbed rice (nasi ulam) mixed with aromatic herbs and served with sambal belacan and fried fish.
Old kopitiam on Penang Hill offering Hainanese kopi, soft-boiled eggs, and charcoal toast since the 1940s.
Crispy fried oyster omelet with fresh oysters, eggs, and tangy chili sauce at the popular Batu Lanchang wet market.
Pure vegetarian banana leaf restaurant offering unlimited rice with rotating curries, papadam, and pickles.
Famous night market stall on Kimberley Street. Thick, smoky char koay teow in a fragrant wok.
A well-known chain serving consistent mamak-style nasi kandar, roti canai, and mee goreng mamak round the clock.
The most iconic hawker centre in Penang, located by the sea. Home to dozens of famous stalls including Char Koay Teow, Assam Laksa, and Pasembur.
Clear herbal pork rib soup (bak kut teh) simmered for hours. Uniquely Penang-style — lighter and more aromatic than the KL version.
Humble warung serving home-style Malay dishes including rendang ayam, gulai ikan, and sambal petai.
Breakfast hawker stall in Pulau Tikus area. Rich, smoky char koay teow cooked in traditional style on high heat.
Beloved Penang hawker joint famous for its Hainan white coffee, kaya toast, and half-boiled eggs.

stallName: Sri Bahari Road Char Koay Teow
description: A Penang old-timer serving consistently excellent char koay teow with cockles and crispy pork lard.
Grilled cockles on flat rice noodles — a smoky Malay-style version of char koay teow with thick dark sauce.
Vibrant asam laksa from a traditional Malay kampung house. Sweet-sour-spicy fish broth with mint leaves.
Old-school Cantonese restaurant serving clay pot rice, crispy roast pork, and hong shao rou.
Beloved mamak stall near Masjid Negeri offering Mee Goreng Mamak with egg and prawns in thick savory sauce.
World-renowned Penang Assam Laksa at the historic Chow Rasta Market — sour mackerel broth with rice noodles.
A sibling to the Chow Rasta Market stalls. Fiery wok-hei char koay teow with fresh clams.
Modern cafe serving fusion Penang flavors — try the Char Koay Teow Pizza and Laksa Carbonara.
One of Penang's most talked-about nasi lemak spots. Creamy coconut rice wrapped in banana leaf with fiery sambal.
A popular late-night kebab and shawarma stall at Gurney Drive serving juicy grilled meats with garlic sauce.
Authentic South Indian vegetarian restaurant. Famous for unlimited banana leaf rice with rotating daily curries.
Claypot chicken rice cooked over charcoal, with generous amounts of ginger, dark soy, and salted fish.
Street hawker dish unique to Penang. Whole squid stuffed with noodles, cooked in rich dark gravy.

stallName: Kim Leng Kafe Chee Cheong Fun
description: Smooth steamed rice rolls (chee cheong fun) drizzled with sweet prawn paste, sesame oil, and chili.
Famous Musang King and D24 durian straight from the orchards of Balik Pulau. Season from May to August.
Legendary mamak restaurant famous for its rich, creamy chicken kapitan curry and fluffy paratha.
Open-air grilled fish restaurant. Choose your fish, pick a sambal, and enjoy it fresh off the charcoal.
Rich, dark prawn broth Hokkien noodles in the Pulau Tikus neighborhood. Deep, sweet flavor with pork ribs.
Open-air food court in George Town with over 30 stalls serving authentic Penang favorites every evening.
A central George Town hawker centre. Must-try is the Cantonese roast duck and popiah stall.
Beloved morning kopitiam famous for strong Hainanese black coffee paired with kaya butter toast.
Authentic Kelantanese nasi dagang with fragrant glutinous rice served with tuna fish curry and coconut sambal.
Traditional Malay kuih bahulu baked in copper molds over charcoal. Crispy outside, soft inside.
Classic Penang curry mee with a milky, coconut-rich broth topped with tofu puff, shrimp, and blood cockles.
Authentic Hong Kong-style dim sum served from bamboo steamers — har gau, siew mai, and char siu bao.
Humble round-the-clock mamak in Butterworth popular for roti canai, thosai, and teh tarik.
Fresh spring rolls (popiah) stuffed with braised turnip, carrots, bean sprouts, prawns, and sweet sauce.
Five-spice meat rolls, prawn fritters, and tofu — served with sweet, thick dipping sauce.
Traditional Malay mee rebus with thick, hearty sweet potato gravy topped with boiled egg and green chili.
Heritage shop selling cooling herbal teas and Penang-style herbal jelly. Great for a hot afternoon.
Flat, wide egg noodles tossed in chili-vinegar sauce with minced pork, fish cake, and fish balls.
Fresh river crabs cooked in rich, creamy coconut turmeric gravy (masak lemak). Seasonal freshness guaranteed.
Penang Asam Laksa with a punchy, sour-spicy broth. Popular with tourists and locals at Chow Rasta Market.
Cantonese-style roast duck with crispy skin served over steamed rice, with house-made plum sauce.
Peranakan heritage cooking with dishes like Perut Ikan, Itik Tim, and Inchi Cabin. A Penang treasure.
Blue-hued herb rice (nasi kerabu) from Kelantan, served with fried chicken, salted egg, and raw veggies.
Blanched squid and kangkung (water spinach) drizzled with thick, sweet-spicy peanut sauce.
Authentic Penang Assam Laksa with generous fish flakes, sliced cucumber, torch ginger, and black prawn paste.
Modern Starbucks cafe inside a heritage building on Beach Street. Great for cold brews and iced lattes.
Crispy banana fritters deep fried in fresh batter. Available in sweet and savory varieties. Always sold out by noon.
Slow-braised duck in fragrant spiced soy broth, served with hard-boiled eggs and steamed rice.
Fragrant tomato-infused rice served with chicken kurma, acar, and boiled egg. A Penang wedding feast staple.
Famous Butterworth nasi lemak with a standout sambal sotong (squid). Rich, spicy, and generous portions.
Tender pork slices, minced pork dumplings, and liver in an umami-rich broth over thin noodles.
Massive red snapper fish head in a rich, spicy curry broth. Famously served in a huge claypot.
Crispy, stuffed murtabak filled with ground mutton, onion, and egg served with spicy curry dipping sauce.
Soft, fluffy steamed bao stuffed with juicy barbecued pork filling. Made fresh daily since 1962.
Iconic cafe on Penang Road serving hearty Penang-style noodle dishes and kaya toast breakfast.

stallName: Pulau Tikus Market Yong Tau Fu
description: Stuffed tofu and vegetable items in a clear broth — choose your favorites from a wide selection.
Fragrant Arab-style rice (Nasi Arab) topped with chicken or lamb, saffron, and raisins. Popular for Friday lunch.
A hawker legend along Jalan Kelawei, known for a moist but slightly drier style of char koay teow.
Authentic North Indian restaurant specializing in clay oven-baked tandoori dishes and naan varieties.
Multi-stall kopitiam with famous wonton soup, roast pork rice, and char siew. A George Town institution.
Hawker-style mamak mee near Fort Cornwallis. Quick, affordable mee goreng and maggi goreng.
Mobile cart selling Penang street snacks: oh chien (oyster omelet), fried carrot cake, and satay.
Trendy bubble tea cafe serving handcrafted boba with fruit teas, milk teas, and fresh toppings.
Specialty coffee shop in a restored shophouse. Serving pour-over, cold brew, and Penang-inspired pastries.
Dry hand-pulled noodles topped with spicy house chili sauce, minced pork, poached egg, and anchovies.
Bustling Jelutong night market every Thursday. A treasure trove of Malay kuihs, grilled meats, and desserts.
Paper-thin crispy prata (roti canai) served with dhal, fish curry, and mutton curry late into the night.
Artisanal Penang-inspired ice cream flavors including durian, cendol, and kaya toast.
Multi-hawker food court with legendary stalls including famous duck rice, carrot cake, and fresh fruits.
Charcoal-toasted bread with kaya jam and butter, served with soft-boiled eggs and Hainanese coffee.
